fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 29-year-old Hispanic male with schizoaffective disorder for routine check.
fields.Subjective: Patient presented on time appearing well and relaxed. Reports taking his risperidone 4 mg nightly consistently using bedtime routine, never misses doses. States he is doing very well. No hallucinations for past five months. No paranoid ideation. Sleep excellent at 8 hours nightly. Appetite healthy, weight stable at 171 lbs. Working full-time as delivery driver, enjoys the independence and exercise. Mood 'good, happy with how things are going' rated 8/10. Lives independently in studio apartment. Dating someone he met through mutual friends, relationship going well. Enjoys soccer league on weekends. Attends therapy monthly for check-ins. Two years sober from alcohol and marijuana. Rebuilding relationship with family, regular dinners with parents weekly. Feels optimistic about future. No safety concerns, denies SI/HI. (Evaluated by Dr. Sofia Ramirez, MD, Psychiatrist)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Casually dressed in work uniform, good grooming, relaxed

Laboratory_Data.Recent_Lab_Results: CBC and metabolic panel all within normal limits.

fields.Psychosocial_Context: Living independently; employed full-time; two years sober; in relationship; reconnected with family; active lifestyle.
Assessment_and_Plan: Excellent medication adherence and symptom control.
fields.Author: Note written by Dr. Sofia Ramirez, MD, Psychiatrist — Latino Mental Health Initiative, 2341 Mission Street, San Antonio, TX 78205, Phone: (555) 772-4491, Fax: (555) 772-4492
